main_turf.bias_notes: A completely flat track with a highly unique figure-of-eight layout. In races over 1m 2f, horses must navigate both left-handed and right-handed turns. The 5F and 6F sprints are relatively straight but feature a slight dog-leg right.
main_turf.post_bias: In large field sprints (5F-6F), high numbers (stands-side) historically hold an advantage. When the ground is soft, the entire field will usually drift to the stands-side rail searching for better ground.

handicapping_angles.the_figure_eight_agility: Because of the sharp turns in both directions, large, long-striding horses often struggle to maintain their rhythm. Heavily upgrade smaller, nimble, athletic horses with proven form on sharp tracks.
handicapping_angles.flat_speed_advantage: Unlike the stiff, grueling uphill finishes of Carlisle or Leicester, Windsor is totally flat. On Good/Firm ground, front-runners and prominent racers ('E' and 'P' types) are extremely difficult to catch.
handicapping_angles.monday_night_specialists: Windsor is the premier track for Monday night summer racing in the UK. Certain southern-based trainers heavily target these meetings, and 'horses for courses' is a massive factor here.
